channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: After years of speculation, a reporter claims to have uncovered the founder of Bitcoin
item.description: After years of speculation, New York Times reporter John Carreyrou explains why he thinks he identified the true founder of Bitcoin.
item.content:encoded: After years of speculation, New York Times reporter John Carreyrou explains why he thinks he identified the true founder of Bitcoin.
item.dc:creator: Mary Louise Kelly

item.title: They went seeking paradise. Instead, they found a living hell
item.description: From 1959 to 1984, some 93,000 people set out on an exodus. They were promised paradise, but ended up trapped in North Korea. Survivors have won an initial victory, but still face an uphill battle.
item.content:encoded: From 1959 to 1984, some 93,000 people set out on an exodus. They were promised paradise, but ended up trapped in North Korea. Survivors have won an initial victory, but still face an uphill battle.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Kuhn

item.title: An Israeli and a Palestinian share their journey for Middle East peace in a new book
item.description: The war in Gaza has hardened positions across the Middle East. But two men say it brought them closer together and convinced them that the "future is peace." That's the title of their new book.
item.content:encoded: The war in Gaza has hardened positions across the Middle East. But two men say it brought them closer together and convinced them that the "future is peace." That's the title of their new book.
item.dc:creator: Michele Kelemen

item.title: No phones nor radios, but lots of weapons — how Hezbollah has adapted in war
item.description: In 2024, Israel killed Hezbollah's top leaders and is thought to have decimated its arsenal. So how is the Iran-backed group still firing thousands of rockets into Israel?
item.content:encoded: In 2024, Israel killed Hezbollah's top leaders and is thought to have decimated its arsenal. So how is the Iran-backed group still firing thousands of rockets into Israel?
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: Tensions between President Trump and Pope Leo XIV on display
item.description: President Trump criticized Pope Leo XIV on Sunday. In a post on social media and comments to reporters, Trump said the American-born Pope is weak on crime and terrible for foreign policy.
item.content:encoded: President Trump criticized Pope Leo XIV on Sunday. In a post on social media and comments to reporters, Trump said the American-born Pope is weak on crime and terrible for foreign policy.
item.dc:creator: Jason DeRose

item.title: Remembering NPR audio engineer Brian Jarboe
item.description: We take a moment to remember former NPR audio engineer Brian Jarboe, who died on Monday. As a colleague, he was kind, agile and skilled in the studio — and he always had a guitar pick on him.
item.content:encoded: We take a moment to remember former NPR audio engineer Brian Jarboe, who died on Monday. As a colleague, he was kind, agile and skilled in the studio — and he always had a guitar pick on him.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Gray

item.title: A U.S. military blockade of Iran's ports takes effect. What does that mean?
item.description: A U.S. blockade of Iranian ports has gone into effect Monday, while President Trump claims Iran wants to make a deal.
item.content:encoded: A U.S. blockade of Iranian ports has gone into effect Monday, while President Trump claims Iran wants to make a deal.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Northam

item.title: Sexual misconduct on Capitol Hill: The problem that won't go away
item.description: NPR'S Mary Louise speaks with PBS NewsHour correspondent Lisa Desjardins about sexual misconduct allegations against Rep. Eric Swalwell and the pattern of such allegations against members of Congress.
item.content:encoded: NPR'S Mary Louise speaks with PBS NewsHour correspondent Lisa Desjardins about sexual misconduct allegations against Rep. Eric Swalwell and the pattern of such allegations against members of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Dorning

item.title: Where are American Catholics on Trump's fight with the Pope?
item.description: NPR's Ailsa Chang speaks with conservative commentator and podcaster Michael Knowles about President Trump and Pope Leo XIV.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ailsa Chang speaks with conservative commentator and podcaster Michael Knowles about President Trump and Pope Leo XIV.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Bartlam

item.title: Justin Bieber — and his laptop — return at Coachella
item.description: Justin Bieber made a high-profile return to public performance at the Coachella Music Festival last weekend. But it's how he did it that has fans talking.
item.content:encoded: Justin Bieber made a high-profile return to public performance at the Coachella Music Festival last weekend. But it's how he did it that has fans talking.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Gomez Sarmiento
